name: Flipkart Product Catalog — DataLoader
scenario: Flipkart's product listing page loads in 3 seconds because each product triggers separate DB queries for seller and reviews. A senior engineer profiled it: 50 products = 101 DB queries. Fix it with @BatchMapping and verify with query count assertions.

core.description: Product @QueryMapping: products(category: String) returns List<Product> with id, name, sellerId fields.
@BatchMapping seller(List<Product>): finds all sellers in ONE batch query. Returns Map<Product, Seller>. Uses sellerRepo.findByIdIn(ids).
@BatchMapping reviews(List<Product>): finds all reviews in ONE batch query. Returns Map<Product, List<Review>>. Groups by productId.
SellerRepository and ReviewRepository: each has a findByIdIn / findByProductIdIn method with AtomicInteger query counter.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): 5+ products returned, seller batch = 1 query, review batch = 1 query, all products have valid seller (not 'Unknown'). All pass.
README: explains N+1 problem (query count without vs with @BatchMapping for 50 products).

bonus.description: PerformanceComparator @Service: simulate naive (N+1 queries) vs batch (2 queries) for N=50. Assert speedup > 10×. Test: 3 assertions on query counts and latency.
CachingDataLoader @Service: Map<String, T> cache per request. load(id) returns cached if present. Test: same id twice = 1 DB hit, different id = 2 DB hits.
QueryComplexityAnalyzer @Service: calculateComplexity(depth, breadth). Rejects depth=4 breadth=10. Allows depth=1 breadth=10. Test: 3 assertions.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. @BatchMapping method signature: must accept List<ParentType> and return Map<ParentType, FieldType> (or Map<ParentType, List<FieldType>> for list fields). Spring for GraphQL collects all parent instances from the query, then calls your @BatchMapping once with all of them. findByIdIn vs findByProductIdIn: seller uses findByIdIn(List<sellerId>) — the ID is on the Product. Review uses findByProductIdIn(List<productId>) — the ID is on the Review. Deduplication: use distinct() or a Set to avoid duplicate IDs in the batch query (same seller may appear on multiple products). Map grouping: collect the batch results into Map<String, T> by ID, then stream products to create the final Map<Product, T>.
